Practical: Build, Debug and Validate

PHASE 2: LINUX BASICS

On this page

Linux System Programming with C++ ​

Welcome to this documentation site for learning Linux system programming with C++.

This guide is written in a beginner-friendly way and focuses on practical topics you will actually use while building real projects.

What you will learn ​

  • build systems with Makefiles and CMake
  • debug and release compiler flags
  • sanitizers like ASan, UBSan, and TSan
  • memory checking with Valgrind
  • clean project structure for C++ development
  • project-based phases to practice what you learn

How to use this site ​

You can read the pages in order if you are learning from scratch, or use them as a quick reference while working on your own Linux C++ projects.

Getting Started ​

Start with Phase 1 — Build Tooling to learn the foundation:

  • how to build your code
  • how to debug safely
  • how to check for memory issues
  • how to structure your project properly

Goal of this documentation ​

The goal of this site is to help you learn Linux system programming with C++ in a way that is:

  • simple
  • practical
  • beginner-friendly
  • easy to revisit as a reference
